hero.title: Lip Sync
hero.description: AI lip sync generator. Sync any audio to any video automatically. Perfect for dubbing, multilingual content, and creative revoicing.
hero.inputPlaceholder: Upload video and audio to sync

steps.title: Upload your source video
steps.description: Start with a video where the speaker or character is clearly visible — such as talking-head clips, explainers, or performance shots.

steps.title: Provide new audio or text for TTS
steps.description: Upload a target voice track or provide text for AI-generated speech in the language and style you need for dubbing or localization.

steps.title: Generate AI lip-synced video
steps.description: Run the Lip Sync workflow so AI aligns mouth shapes and timing in the original footage to match the new audio naturally.

steps.title: Export for localization, dubbing, or creative edits
steps.description: Review results, tweak inputs if needed, and export final videos for multilingual versions, remixes, or personalized content.

whoIsFor.title: For teams repurposing and localizing video content

items.question: What is AI Lip Sync?
items.answer: An AI workflow that automatically syncs lip movements in a video to match new audio. Great for dubbing, language localization, and creative revoicing.

items.question: What do I need to provide?
items.answer: Upload a video with visible faces and the audio you want to sync to it. The AI adjusts lip movements to match the new audio.

items.question: Does it work for multiple languages?
items.answer: Yes. You can sync audio in any language to the video. It's commonly used for dubbing content into different languages.

items.question: What are typical use cases?
items.answer: Dubbing videos for international audiences, creating multilingual content, revoicing characters, and syncing dialogue for creative projects.

items.question: How accurate is the lip sync?
items.answer: Quality depends on video quality, face visibility, and audio clarity. Best results come from clear frontal faces and clean audio tracks.

items.question: How does pricing work?
items.answer: Credits are consumed based on video length and processing complexity. Test with shorter clips first to find optimal settings.
